dataset_name: Sports Reference QB Passing Export Slice - 2014 to 2025

review_summary.important_caveat: This JSON contains only the rows present in the uploaded Sports Reference export slice. Because the rank range is 201-400, do not treat this as a complete 2014-2025 QB universe unless additional export pages are provided.

cleaning_notes: Removed the blank/helper row from the Sports Reference export.
Removed one duplicate completion column caused by the export layout; kept pass_cmp from the Passing section.
Converted numeric-looking fields into integers/floats where appropriate.
Filtered to rows where position begins with QB.

schema.rank: Sports Reference row rank from the uploaded export slice. This file starts at rank 201, so ranks are not a complete league ranking.
schema.player: Quarterback/player name as displayed in the export.
schema.team: Team abbreviation from source. Multi-team rows may use aggregate/team code from source if present.
schema.pass_cmp: Pass completions. Duplicate pre-header completion column was removed; this is the Passing section completion value.
schema.success_pct: Passing success rate percentage from source.

claude_instructions: Use this as a cleaned data package for QB passing analysis.
Do not assume missing QB seasons are low-performing or absent from the league; they may simply be outside the uploaded rank slice.
When ranking or comparing players, explicitly state that comparisons are within this uploaded slice unless more complete files are provided.
Use adjusted_net_yards_per_attempt, pass_rating, success_pct, pass_td_pct, pass_int_pct, sack_pct, and volume fields together rather than relying on raw yards alone.
For modeling NFL QB quality, account for era, games started, and volume before comparing efficiency metrics.
